name: Cinema Platform
description:
Merged metadata descriptions from all Cinema Platform services.

# billboard-service
Enterprise-grade showtime and seat reservation microservice for the Cinema Platform.
Handles scheduling lifecycle, business-rule validation, catalog integration via gRPC,
Redis caching, and optional Kafka event publishing.

# catalog-service
Enterprise-grade catalog microservice for cinemas, theaters, movies, and seats.
Exposes REST and gRPC APIs with Clean Architecture, optional Kafka events,
Redis caching, and JWT role-based access control.

# concession-service
Cinema concession microservice for products, categories, combo meals, and promotional
campaigns. REST API v2 for clients; gRPC ConcessionCatalogService for peer services.
DDD/CQRS with Redis caching, JWT RBAC, and Docker-orchestrated deployment.

# employee-service
HR and workforce microservice for the Cinema Platform. Manages employee records,
work schedules, and vacation requests with JWT role-based access, Redis caching,
and async PostgreSQL persistence. Deployed on AWS EC2 with Amazon RDS and Upstash Redis.

# notification-service
Centralized multi-channel notification microservice for the Cinema Platform.
Delivers Email (SMTP/Jinja2), SMS (Twilio), Push, and In-App notifications
via REST API and Kafka event consumption with MongoDB history and deduplication.

# payment-service
Cinema platform payment microservice — tickets, concessions, merchandise,
subscriptions, and wallet credits. Stripe gateway, PostgreSQL persistence,
Kafka events, role-split REST APIs (customer/staff/admin), and payment method catalog.

# ticket-service
Cinema ticket microservice — purchase, cancel, and use tickets; seat availability
and quotes from Mongo billboard replica; PostgreSQL ticket store; Kafka sync;
outbound gRPC to payment and billboard services.

# user-service
Cinema platform user microservice — registration, JWT sessions, 2FA (TOTP),
profiles, admin user management, Redis session store, Kafka events, and gRPC
directory lookups for other services.

# wallet-service
Digital wallet microservice for the Cinema Platform. Manages customer balances,
credit top-ups, ticket/concession payments, transaction history, and staff
reporting. Deployed on AWS EC2 with Amazon RDS and Upstash Redis; optional
Kafka event publishing and gRPC placeholder for future inter-service RPC.

problemStatement.problemDescription:
Combined problem descriptions from all Cinema Platform services.

# billboard-service
Cinema platforms need a centralized service to schedule showtimes across theaters,
track seat availability in real time, enforce booking business rules, and coordinate
with catalog and payment services. Without a dedicated billboard service, scheduling
logic leaks into multiple apps, seat state becomes inconsistent, and integrations
are brittle.

# catalog-service
Cinema platforms need a single source of truth for movies, cinema locations,
theater screens, and seat layouts. Downstream services (billboard, ticket, payment)
require fast, consistent catalog lookups — often via gRPC — while admins need
rich CRUD with business-rule enforcement and audit-friendly soft deletes.

# concession-service
Theaters need a unified backend for concession inventory — snacks, beverages,
combos, and time-limited promotions — with low-latency reads for POS systems
and integration with ticketing/loyalty microservices. Monolithic catalogs lack
flexible pricing, promotion rules, and protocol choice (REST vs gRPC).

# employee-service
Cinema chains need a dedicated service to track staff across departments (projection,
concessions, ticketing, security, etc.), manage shift schedules, and handle vacation
approvals. Without a centralized employee service, HR data fragments across spreadsheets
and ad-hoc tools, schedule conflicts go undetected, and authorization for workforce
APIs becomes inconsistent across the platform.

# notification-service
Each Cinema Platform microservice historically handled its own emails, SMS, and
in-app alerts. That leads to inconsistent templates, scattered SMTP/Twilio credentials,
duplicate deliveries on retried Kafka events, and poor operational visibility when
notifications fail.

# payment-service
Cinema purchases span tickets, concessions, merchandise, subscriptions, and wallet
top-ups across multiple services. A dedicated payment service must orchestrate
provider integration (Stripe), enforce refund rules, emit events, and expose
role-appropriate APIs while keeping PCI-sensitive data off the platform database.

# ticket-service
Cinema platforms need reliable ticket purchasing, seat reservations, and lifecycle
management while integrating with billboard showtimes and payment authorization.
High concurrency requires atomic seat handling with authoritative validation.

# user-service
Cinema platforms need centralized user management for registration, authentication,
profiles, and authorization across microservices. Legacy systems often lack secure
session management, MFA, and event-driven lifecycle notifications.

# wallet-service
Cinema platforms need a centralized wallet service so customers can preload
credit, pay for tickets and concessions, and review transaction history.
Without a dedicated wallet bounded context, payment logic leaks into order
and user services, balances become inconsistent, and financial audit trails
are incomplete.

solutionList.title: Showtime Domain Model
solutionList.description: Rich domain entity with encapsulated validation for price ($3–$50), duration (30–300 min), schedule window, and lifecycle transitions.

solutionList.title: Seat Reservation System
solutionList.description: Per-seat take/leave operations with timestamps, user IDs, and transaction tracking; available-seat counts derived at the showtime level.

solutionList.title: Catalog Service Integration
solutionList.description: gRPC port/adapter to validate movie and theater existence and fetch theater capacity before persisting showtimes.

solutionList.title: Async Infrastructure
solutionList.description: Async SQLAlchemy + Redis caching + optional Kafka publisher, all wired through FastAPI dependency injection and use-case containers.

solutionList.title: Multi-Domain Architecture
solutionList.description: Separate bounded contexts for Movies, Cinemas, Theaters, and Seats with shared infrastructure (auth, pagination, events, persistence).

solutionList.title: Domain Business Rules
solutionList.description: Theater capacity validation by type, movie exhibition date logic, maintenance mode, and seat type classification in domain entities.

solutionList.title: Dual API Surface
solutionList.description: REST for admin/portal operations; embedded gRPC server for sub-30 ms catalog queries from peer microservices.

solutionList.title: Soft Delete Pattern
solutionList.description: deleted_at on all catalog entities with explicit restore endpoints for cinemas and theaters.

solutionList.title: Hexagonal Architecture
solutionList.description: Domain logic isolated from SQLAlchemy, FastAPI, and Redis via ports/adapters; testable use cases per bounded context.

solutionList.title: Dual Protocol (REST + gRPC)
solutionList.description: REST `/api/v2/*` for external clients; ConcessionCatalogService gRPC for bulk product/combo/promotion lookups by ID.

solutionList.title: Redis Caching Layer
solutionList.description: init_cache() on FastAPI lifespan for sub-100 ms hot reads and reduced DB load.

solutionList.title: CQRS Use Cases
solutionList.description: Separate commands (create/update/delete) and queries (search, get-by-id) with pagination across all list endpoints.

solutionList.title: Employee Domain Model
solutionList.description: Rich `Employee` entity with state transitions (terminate, suspend, reactivate, promote) and validation for email format and termination dates.

solutionList.title: Schedule Management
solutionList.description: Per-employee work schedules with date/time windows, shift types, and conflict validation (start_time must precede end_time).

solutionList.title: Vacation Workflow
solutionList.description: Vacation requests with PENDING → APPROVED/REJECTED transitions, reviewer tracking, and manager comments.

solutionList.title: Async Infrastructure
solutionList.description: Async SQLAlchemy + optional Upstash Redis cache, FastAPI dependency injection, and use-case containers per bounded context (employees, schedules, vacations).

solutionList.title: Event-Driven Processing
solutionList.description: Kafka consumer on notification.incoming, cinema.user-service.events, and wallet.events — auto-creates and sends notifications with event_id deduplication.

solutionList.title: Multi-Channel Delivery
solutionList.description: Single domain model routed to Email (SMTP), SMS (Twilio), Push, or In-App via SendingService port/adapter.

solutionList.title: MongoDB Document Store
solutionList.description: Async Motor persistence for full notification lifecycle, filters, and pagination by user, type, channel, and attention status.

solutionList.title: Attention Tracking
solutionList.description: Flags important or failed notifications (OPEN → ACKNOWLEDGED → RESOLVED) for operational follow-up.

solutionList.title: Domain-Driven Payment Aggregate
solutionList.description: Payment aggregate root with lifecycle (pending → processing → completed/failed), refund rules, and domain events (PaymentCreated, PaymentCompleted, etc.).

solutionList.title: Event-Driven Communication
solutionList.description: Kafka publishers for payment.events; consumers on payment.incoming, billboard.events, and wallet.events when enabled.

solutionList.title: Multi-Provider Gateway
solutionList.description: PaymentGateway port with Stripe adapter; wallet and catalog payment methods.

solutionList.title: Role-Split REST APIs
solutionList.description: Customer self-service, staff operations (show revenue, refunds), and admin overrides/search under separate route prefixes.

solutionList.title: RESTful Ticket API
solutionList.description: Command/query split routers for buy, cancel, use, search, and quotes at /api/v2/tickets.

solutionList.title: Mongo Billboard Replica
solutionList.description: Read-heavy showtime, theater, and seat data replicated from Kafka billboard.events.

solutionList.title: PostgreSQL Ticket Store
solutionList.description: Authoritative ticket records, transactions, and seat assignments.

solutionList.title: gRPC External Assertions
solutionList.description: Outbound clients to GRPC_BILLBOARD_TARGET and GRPC_PAYMENT_TARGET when configured.

solutionList.title: QR Confirmation Payload
solutionList.description: TicketBuyedResponse includes QR data for door scanning.

solutionList.title: Hexagonal Architecture
solutionList.description: Domain, application, and infrastructure layers with ports/adapters per bounded context (users, auth, profile).

solutionList.title: JWT Session Management
solutionList.description: Stateless access tokens plus Redis-backed refresh tokens for logout and refresh flows.

solutionList.title: Two-Factor Authentication
solutionList.description: TOTP with QR enrollment and step-up login via /auth/2FA/access.

solutionList.title: Event-Driven Lifecycle
solutionList.description: Kafka publisher for user events when KAFKA_ENABLED (signup, activation, etc.).

solutionList.title: Dual Protocol
solutionList.description: REST for clients; gRPC UsersService (GetUserById/Email/Phone) for internal callers.

solutionList.title: Wallet Aggregate
solutionList.description: `Wallet` entity owns balance mutations and transaction creation — add_credit, buy_product, with WalletDomainValidator enforcing currency and amount rules.

solutionList.title: Three API Surfaces
solutionList.description: Staff routes (`/api/v2/wallets`), customer routes (`/api/v2/wallets/users`), and management summary (`/api/v2/wallets/management`).

solutionList.title: Payment Orchestration
solutionList.description: Add-credit and pay use cases coordinate with PaymentInternalService port (gRPC stub today) before persisting wallet state.

solutionList.title: Cloud-Native Deployment
solutionList.description: Docker on EC2, PostgreSQL on RDS, Upstash Redis for cache validation, optional Kafka for wallet.events topic.

decisions.title: FastAPI over Flask/Django
decisions.problem: Need async-capable, high-performance REST with automatic OpenAPI.
decisions.solution: FastAPI with native async, Pydantic validation, and built-in /docs.
decisions.outcome: Low-latency handlers, type-safe DTOs, self-documenting API.
alternatives: Flask with async extensions (less mature typing)

decisions.title: gRPC for Catalog Integration
decisions.problem: Low-latency service-to-service validation of movies and theaters.
decisions.solution: gRPC + protobuf CatalogGateway adapter.
decisions.outcome: Sub-30 ms catalog lookups under normal network conditions.

decisions.title: Business Rules in Domain Entity
decisions.problem: Validation logic duplicated across use cases and API layer.
decisions.solution: Centralize in Showtime.validate_business_logic() and lifecycle methods.
decisions.outcome: Testable, reusable rules independent of HTTP framework.

decisions.title: Embedded gRPC Server
decisions.problem: Peer services need fast catalog validation without HTTP overhead.
decisions.solution: Start grpc.aio.Server in FastAPI lifespan alongside REST.
decisions.outcome: Single deployable unit; sub-30 ms lookups for movie/theater checks.

decisions.title: Multi-Package Domains
decisions.problem: Movies, cinemas, theaters have different rules but share infra.
decisions.solution: app/movies, app/cinema, app/theater packages with shared core.
decisions.outcome: Clear boundaries; independent evolution per domain.

decisions.title: Theater Capacity in Domain
decisions.problem: Invalid capacities must be rejected before DB write.
decisions.solution: CAPACITY_RULES ClassVar on Theater entity with validate_capacity().
decisions.outcome: Testable rules; consistent errors across REST and future gRPC mutations.

decisions.title: CQRS over anemic services
decisions.problem: Read and write shapes differ (search vs create).
decisions.solution: Explicit Command/Query types per use case.
decisions.outcome: Clearer tests; optimized query objects for pagination.
alternatives: Single service class per entity (more coupling)

decisions.title: Separate gRPC container
decisions.problem: Gunicorn workers not ideal for long-lived gRPC server.
decisions.solution: docker-entrypoint serve-grpc runs app.grpc.server standalone.
decisions.outcome: Independent scaling and failure isolation for RPC.
alternatives: Embed gRPC in FastAPI lifespan (catalog pattern)

decisions.title: UUID value objects
decisions.problem: String IDs prone to format errors across services.
decisions.solution: ProductId/ComboId/PromotionId with from_string validation.
decisions.outcome: Consistent parsing in REST and gRPC layers.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis over ElastiCache
decisions.problem: Need managed Redis without VPC cluster ops for a portfolio-scale deployment.
decisions.solution: Use Upstash serverless Redis with TLS URL in REDIS_URL; graceful fallback if unavailable.
decisions.outcome: Lower ops overhead; cache optional so API remains functional without Redis.
alternatives: AWS ElastiCache in same VPC as EC2
In-memory cache (rejected — not shared across replicas)

decisions.title: Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL
decisions.problem: Durable workforce data with backups and SSL.
decisions.solution: RDS PostgreSQL with POSTGRES_SSL_MODE=require from EC2.
decisions.outcome: Managed backups, patching, and connection endpoint separate from app host.

decisions.title: EC2 + Docker Compose (prod)
decisions.problem: Simple deployment for a single-region cinema platform demo.
decisions.solution: docker-compose.prod.yml runs app container only; external RDS and Upstash via .env.
decisions.outcome: Portable image; same Dockerfile for local and cloud.

decisions.title: MongoDB over PostgreSQL
decisions.problem: Notification documents vary by channel with nested content/recipient.
decisions.solution: Document store with flexible schema and Motor async driver.
decisions.outcome: Fast writes and rich filter queries without heavy joins.

decisions.title: In-process Kafka consumer
decisions.problem: Need event-driven notifications without separate worker deploy initially.
decisions.solution: asyncio tasks in FastAPI lifespan calling kafka-python poll loop.
decisions.outcome: Single deployable unit; consumer stops cleanly on shutdown.

decisions.title: Jinja2 email templates
decisions.problem: Consistent branding across notification types.
decisions.solution: Template map per NotificationType in EmailService.
decisions.outcome: HTML emails with injected template_data from content.data.

decisions.title: PostgreSQL for payments
decisions.problem: ACID transactions, audit trail, and relational reporting.
decisions.solution: Async SQLAlchemy with Alembic migrations on cinema_payment DB.
decisions.outcome: Strong consistency for money movement and admin summaries.

decisions.title: Separate gRPC container
decisions.problem: Isolate long-lived gRPC server from HTTP workers.
decisions.solution: app-grpc service with serve-grpc entrypoint on port 50051.
decisions.outcome: Ready for inbound RPC; servicers not registered yet.
alternatives: Embedded gRPC in same process as REST

decisions.title: Stripe as primary gateway
decisions.problem: Card payments and stored payment methods with PCI scope reduction.
decisions.solution: PaymentGateway port with Stripe adapter and token storage.
decisions.outcome: PaymentIntent flow with webhook handler on staff routes.
alternatives: PayPal / Adyen adapters (catalog supports provider enum)

decisions.title: Mongo for billboard replica
decisions.problem: Ticket reads must not synchronously call billboard on every quote.
decisions.solution: Event-synced Mongo collections from Kafka billboard.events.
decisions.outcome: Sub-50ms reads; eventual consistency acceptable for quotes.
alternatives: Synchronous HTTP to billboard on every request

decisions.title: PostgreSQL for tickets
decisions.problem: ACID seat assignment and ticket lifecycle auditing.
decisions.solution: Relational ticket and seat tables with Alembic migrations.
decisions.outcome: Strong consistency for sold seats and status transitions.
alternatives: Tickets only in Mongo (weaker transactional guarantees)

decisions.title: CQRS routers
decisions.problem: Write operations need stricter limits and different OpenAPI tags.
decisions.solution: Separate command and query APIRouter modules, same prefix.
decisions.outcome: Clear Swagger grouping; independent rate limit policies.

decisions.title: Redis for refresh tokens
decisions.problem: Need revocable sessions with logout-all support.
decisions.solution: Store refresh tokens in Redis; access tokens remain stateless JWT.
decisions.outcome: Fast invalidation without DB round-trips on every API call.

decisions.title: Integer user IDs
decisions.problem: Legacy schema and sequential admin tooling.
decisions.solution: Auto-increment integer PK on users table.
decisions.outcome: Simpler joins in ticket service; differs from UUID-based services.

decisions.title: Separate gRPC container
decisions.problem: Directory lookups should not compete with HTTP worker pool.
decisions.solution: app-grpc with serve-grpc entrypoint on port 50051.
decisions.outcome: Independent scaling; verify app.grpc module path matches sources.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis over ElastiCache
decisions.problem: Managed Redis without VPC cluster administration for portfolio deployment.
decisions.solution: Upstash TLS URL in REDIS_URL from EC2 .env.
decisions.outcome: Lower ops overhead; cache optional.

decisions.title: Kafka over RabbitMQ
decisions.problem: Legacy README referenced RabbitMQ; codebase uses kafka-python.
decisions.solution: Optional Kafka publisher with noop fallback when KAFKA_ENABLED=false.
decisions.outcome: Aligns with other cinema platform services; no RabbitMQ dependency.

decisions.title: UUID wallet identifiers
decisions.problem: Distributed user-service integration needs stable external IDs.
decisions.solution: WalletId and UserId value objects wrapping UUID.
decisions.outcome: JWT sub maps directly to user_id for customer routes.

    await init_cache()

async def IsMovieActive(self, request, context):
    async with AsyncSessionLocal() as session:
        repo = SQLAlchemyMovieRepository(session)
        movie = await repo.find_by_id(request.movie_id)
        return catalog_pb2.IsMovieActiveResponse(
            is_active=bool(movie and movie.is_active)
        )

codeExamples.title: Lifespan — gRPC Co-Startup
codeExamples.description: FastAPI lifespan starts gRPC server alongside REST.

files.name: main.py
files.explanation: gRPC server stopped gracefully on shutdown (5s grace).
files.content:
@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    await run_postgres_startup_check(engine)
    grpc_server = create_catalog_grpc_server()
    await grpc_server.start()
    app.state.grpc_server = grpc_server
    yield
    await grpc_server.stop(grace=5)

def mark_as_sent(self, sent_at: Optional[datetime] = None) -> None:
    self.status = NotificationStatus.SENT
    self.sent_at = sent_at or datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    self.failed_at = None
    self.error_details = None

def mark_as_failed(self, error_details: str, ...) -> None:
    self.status = NotificationStatus.FAILED
    self.failed_at = failed_at or datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    self.error_details = error_details

def mark_attention_open(self) -> None:
    self.is_important = True
    self.attention_status = NotificationAttentionStatus.OPEN

FROM python:3.13-slim AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt ./
RUN python -m venv /opt/venv && /opt/venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt

FROM python:3.13-slim AS runtime
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /opt/venv /opt/venv
COPY . .
USER app
EXPOSE 8000
HEALTHCHECK CMD python -c "import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://127.0.0.1:8000/health')"
CMD ["/app/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh", "serve"]

FROM python:3.13-slim AS builder

dockerFiles.content:
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    ports: ["5431:5432"]
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports: ["6378:6379"]
  app-1:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/dockerfile }
  app-2:
    image: wallet-service:local

    ports: ["50051:50051"]

dockerFiles.description: docker-compose.prod.yml — REST container; optional gRPC profile.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  app:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/dockerfile }
    env_file: [../.env]
    ports: ["${API_PORT:-8000}:8000"]
  app-grpc:
    profiles: [grpc]
    command: ["/app/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh", "serve-grpc"]
    ports: ["${GRPC_PORT:-50051}:${GRPC_PORT:-50051}"]
